elevator_pitch: Dishly is a compact countertop appliance that washes a single dish, cup, or utensil in 10 seconds flat. Think of it as the microwave equivalent for dishwashing: instant, one item at a time, no more waiting for a full load.
problem: Traditional dishwashers are batch systems designed for large families in the 1950s. Singles, couples, students, and office workers generate one or two dirty items at a time, leading to piled-up dishes, pre-rinsing rituals, and crusty food that bonds permanently to bowls left waiting for a full load.
solution: Dishly is a countertop single-item dishwasher that uses high-pressure aerated water jets, UV sterilization, and compressed-air drying to clean and sanitize one dish in about 10 seconds. It recycles its own water through a filtration system, uses soap tablets (razor-blade model), and can even ship with custom-designed dishware optimized for a perfect clean every time.
target_market: Singles and couples in apartments, college students, office breakrooms, co-working spaces, and small households who find traditional dishwashers wasteful and inconvenient for their lifestyle.
revenue_model: Hardware sales at $189-$240 per unit, plus recurring revenue from proprietary cleaning tablets (razor-blade model). Premium tier includes custom-designed dishware sets optimized for the appliance. Commercial/office models at higher price points for B2B sales.
key_features: 10-second wash cycle with pressurized aerated water jets
UV sterilization and compressed-air drying built into every cycle
Self-contained water recycling with advanced filtration
Proprietary soap tablet system for effortless, mess-free cleaning
competitive_advantage: No product exists in the single-item rapid dishwashing category. Traditional dishwashers are batch-only and take 1-3 hours. Dishly creates an entirely new appliance category, similar to how the microwave redefined cooking alongside the oven. The razor-blade consumables model and optional custom dishware create strong recurring revenue and ecosystem lock-in.
market_size_estimate: There are roughly 130 million US households, with 37 million single-person households and 35 million two-person households. Adding 5+ million office breakrooms and co-working spaces, the addressable market at $200 per unit is approximately $15 billion, with recurring tablet revenue adding $2-3 billion annually.
go_to_market: Launch via crowdfunding (Kickstarter/Indiegogo) targeting minimalists, tiny-home enthusiasts, and office managers. Leverage viral product demo videos on TikTok and Instagram showing the 10-second wash. Partner with coworking spaces and university housing for bulk B2B orders.
risks: Hardware development and manufacturing complexity could delay time to market and inflate costs
Consumer skepticism that a 10-second cycle can truly sanitize dishes
Established appliance giants (Samsung, Bosch, LG) could copy the concept with greater resources
year_one_milestones: Complete industrial design and working prototype validated by third-party hygiene testing
Raise $500K+ through crowdfunding campaign with 3,000+ pre-orders
Ship first production units and secure 5 B2B pilot programs with co-working spaces or universities
startup_cost_range: $150K - $350K to reach MVP and first production run
